verdict: The chart features excellent flowing spin-sections and solid rhythm tracking, but is held back by a 3-panel lock and several highly uncomfortable 16th-note transitions.

issues.problem: 3-panel lock: Tapping R while both U and D are being held creates an impossible 3-panel step (hand note) for standard feet-only play.
Holding U while tapping L and R as 16th notes forces the single free foot to slide from L to R in just 125ms, which is physically extremely awkward.
Tapping R then immediately jumping to LR as a 16th note creates an uncomfortable single-foot double-tap (jack) directly into a jump. This issue also occurs at 22.372 and 64.391.
Hitting D then immediately jumping to LR as a 16th note forces a highly rushed 125ms foot transition from D to R.
